content.thinking:
The user wants to understand Laravel's authorization system - specifically the central routine that evaluates whether a user can perform an action, and how it's called from different places. This is about the Gate/Policy system in Laravel.

Let me explore the authorization system in Laravel's source code.

content.text: I'll explore Laravel's authorization system to trace the central check and its callers.

input.prompt:
In the Laravel framework source at /home/bench/repos/laravel/src/Illuminate/Auth/Access/, find and read all files. I need to understand:

1. The central method that evaluates whether an ability is allowed (the core check routine)
2. All the public methods that call it (allow, deny, check, any, none, authorize, inspect, etc.)
3. What each caller does with the result — plain boolean, Response object, or exception thrown

Read every file in that directory completely. Also check if there are relevant files in /home/bench/repos/laravel/src/Illuminate/Foundation/Auth/ or any Gate facade. Report the exact method names, file paths, and line numbers for the central evaluation routine and each caller.

1	<?php
2	
3	namespace Illuminate\Auth\Access;
4	
5	use Illuminate\Contracts\Support\Arrayable;
6	use Stringable;
7	
8	class Response implements Arrayable, Stringable
9	{
10	    /**
11	     * Indicates whether the response was allowed.
12	     *
13	     * @var bool
14	     */
15	    protected $allowed;
16	
17	    /**
18	     * The response message.
19	     *
20	     * @var string|null
21	     */
22	    protected $message;
23	
24	    /**
25	     * The response code.
26	     *
27	     * @var mixed
28	     */
29	    protected $code;
30	
31	    /**
32	     * The HTTP response status code.
33	     *
34	     * @var int|null
35	     */
36	    protected $status;
37	
38	    /**
39	     * Create a new response.
40	     *
41	     * @param  bool  $allowed
42	     * @param  string|null  $message
43	     * @param  mixed  $code
44	     */
45	    public function __construct($allowed, $message = '', $code = null)
46	    {
47	        $this->code = $code;
48	        $this->allowed = $allowed;
49	        $this->message = $message;
50	    }
51	
52	    /**
53	     * Create a new "allow" Response.
54	     *
55	     * @param  string|null  $message
56	     * @param  mixed  $code
57	     * @return \Illuminate\Auth\Access\Response
58	     */
59	    public static function allow($message = null, $code = null)
60	    {
61	        return new static(true, $message, $code);
62	    }
63	
64	    /**
65	     * Create a new "deny" Response.
66	     *
67	     * @param  string|null  $message
68	     * @param  mixed  $code
69	     * @return \Illuminate\Auth\Access\Response
70	     */
71	    public static function deny($message = null, $code = null)
72	    {
73	        return new static(false, $message, $code);
74	    }
75	
76	    /**
77	     * Create a new "deny" Response with a HTTP status code.
78	     *
79	     * @param  int  $status
80	     * @param  string|null  $message
81	     * @param  mixed  $code
82	     * @return \Illuminate\Auth\Access\Response
83	     */
84	    public static function denyWithStatus($status, $message = null, $code = null)
85	    {
86	        return static::deny($message, $code)->withStatus($status);
87	    }
88	
89	    /**
90	     * Create a new "deny" Response with a 404 HTTP status code.
91	     *
92	     * @param  string|null  $message
93	     * @param  mixed  $code
94	     * @return \Illuminate\Auth\Access\Response
95	     */
96	    public static function denyAsNotFound($message = null, $code = null)
97	    {
98	        return static::denyWithStatus(404, $message, $code);
99	    }
100	
101	    /**
102	     * Determine if the response was allowed.
103	     *
104	     * @return bool
105	     */
106	    public function allowed()
107	    {
108	        return $this->allowed;
109	    }
110	
111	    /**
112	     * Determine if the response was denied.
113	     *
114	     * @return bool
115	     */
116	    public function denied()
117	    {
118	        return ! $this->allowed();
119	    }
120	
121	    /**
122	     * Get the response message.
123	     *
124	     * @return string|null
125	     */
126	    public function message()
127	    {
128	        return $this->message;
129	    }
130	
131	    /**
132	     * Get the response code / reason.
133	     *
134	     * @return mixed
135	     */
136	    public function code()
137	    {
138	        return $this->code;
139	    }
140	
141	    /**
142	     * Throw authorization exception if response was denied.
143	     *
144	     * @return \Illuminate\Auth\Access\Response
145	     *
146	     * @throws \Illuminate\Auth\Access\AuthorizationException
147	     */
148	    public function authorize()
149	    {
150	        if ($this->denied()) {
151	            throw (new AuthorizationException($this->message(), $this->code()))
152	                ->setResponse($this)
153	                ->withStatus($this->status);
154	        }
155	
156	        return $this;
157	    }
158	
159	    /**
160	     * Set the HTTP response status code.
161	     *
162	     * @param  null|int  $status
163	     * @return $this
164	     */
165	    public function withStatus($status)
166	    {
167	        $this->status = $status;
168	
169	        return $this;
170	    }
171	
172	    /**
173	     * Set the HTTP response status code to 404.
174	     *
175	     * @return $this
176	     */
177	    public function asNotFound()
178	    {
179	        return $this->withStatus(404);
180	    }
181	
182	    /**
183	     * Get the HTTP status code.
184	     *
185	     * @return int|null
186	     */
187	    public function status()
188	    {
189	        return $this->status;
190	    }
191	
192	    /**
193	     * Convert the response to an array.
194	     *
195	     * @return array
196	     */
197	    public function toArray()
198	    {
199	        return [
200	            'allowed' => $this->allowed(),
201	            'message' => $this->message(),
202	            'code' => $this->code(),
203	        ];
204	    }
205	
206	    /**
207	     * Get the string representation of the message.
208	     *
209	     * @return string
210	     */
211	    public function __toString()
212	    {
213	        return (string) $this->message();
214	    }
215	}
216

1	<?php
2	
3	namespace Illuminate\Auth\Access;
4	
5	use Exception;
6	use Throwable;
7	
8	class AuthorizationException extends Exception
9	{
10	    /**
11	     * The authorization response returned by the gate.
12	     *
13	     * @var \Illuminate\Auth\Access\Response
14	     */
15	    protected $response;
16	
17	    /**
18	     * The HTTP response status code.
19	     *
20	     * @var int|null
21	     */
22	    protected $status;
23	
24	    /**
25	     * Create a new authorization exception instance.
26	     *
27	     * @param  string|null  $message
28	     * @param  mixed  $code
29	     * @param  \Throwable|null  $previous
30	     */
31	    public function __construct($message = null, $code = null, ?Throwable $previous = null)
32	    {
33	        parent::__construct($message ?? 'This action is unauthorized.', 0, $previous);
34	
35	        $this->code = $code ?: 0;
36	    }
37	
38	    /**
39	     * Get the response from the gate.
40	     *
41	     * @return \Illuminate\Auth\Access\Response
42	     */
43	    public function response()
44	    {
45	        return $this->response;
46	    }
47	
48	    /**
49	     * Set the response from the gate.
50	     *
51	     * @param  \Illuminate\Auth\Access\Response  $response
52	     * @return $this
53	     */
54	    public function setResponse($response)
55	    {
56	        $this->response = $response;
57	
58	        return $this;
59	    }
60	
61	    /**
62	     * Set the HTTP response status code.
63	     *
64	     * @param  int|null  $status
65	     * @return $this
66	     */
67	    public function withStatus($status)
68	    {
69	        $this->status = $status;
70	
71	        return $this;
72	    }
73	
74	    /**
75	     * Set the HTTP response status code to 404.
76	     *
77	     * @return $this
78	     */
79	    public function asNotFound()
80	    {
81	        return $this->withStatus(404);
82	    }
83	
84	    /**
85	     * Determine if the HTTP status code has been set.
86	     *
87	     * @return bool
88	     */
89	    public function hasStatus()
90	    {
91	        return $this->status !== null;
92	    }
93	
94	    /**
95	     * Get the HTTP status code.
96	     *
97	     * @return int|null
98	     */
99	    public function status()
100	    {
101	        return $this->status;
102	    }
103	
104	    /**
105	     * Create a deny response object from this exception.
106	     *
107	     * @return \Illuminate\Auth\Access\Response
108	     */
109	    public function toResponse()
110	    {
111	        return Response::deny($this->message, $this->code)->withStatus($this->status);
112	    }
113	}
114

1	<?php
2	
3	namespace Illuminate\Auth\Access;
4	
5	trait HandlesAuthorization
6	{
7	    /**
8	     * Create a new access response.
9	     *
10	     * @param  string|null  $message
11	     * @param  mixed  $code
12	     * @return \Illuminate\Auth\Access\Response
13	     */
14	    protected function allow($message = null, $code = null)
15	    {
16	        return Response::allow($message, $code);
17	    }
18	
19	    /**
20	     * Throws an unauthorized exception.
21	     *
22	     * @param  string|null  $message
23	     * @param  mixed  $code
24	     * @return \Illuminate\Auth\Access\Response
25	     */
26	    protected function deny($message = null, $code = null)
27	    {
28	        return Response::deny($message, $code);
29	    }
30	
31	    /**
32	     * Deny with a HTTP status code.
33	     *
34	     * @param  int  $status
35	     * @param  string|null  $message
36	     * @param  int|null  $code
37	     * @return \Illuminate\Auth\Access\Response
38	     */
39	    public function denyWithStatus($status, $message = null, $code = null)
40	    {
41	        return Response::denyWithStatus($status, $message, $code);
42	    }
43	
44	    /**
45	     * Deny with a 404 HTTP status code.
46	     *
47	     * @param  string|null  $message
48	     * @param  int|null  $code
49	     * @return \Illuminate\Auth\Access\Response
50	     */
51	    public function denyAsNotFound($message = null, $code = null)
52	    {
53	        return Response::denyWithStatus(404, $message, $code);
54	    }
55	}

1	<?php
2	
3	namespace Illuminate\Auth\Access\Events;
4	
5	class GateEvaluated
6	{
7	    /**
8	     * The authenticatable model.
9	     *
10	     * @var \Illuminate\Contracts\Auth\Authenticatable|null
11	     */
12	    public $user;
13	
14	    /**
15	     * The ability being evaluated.
16	     *
17	     * @var string
18	     */
19	    public $ability;
20	
21	    /**
22	     * The result of the evaluation.
23	     *
24	     * @var bool|null
25	     */
26	    public $result;
27	
28	    /**
29	     * The arguments given during evaluation.
30	     *
31	     * @var array
32	     */
33	    public $arguments;
34	
35	    /**
36	     * Create a new event instance.
37	     *
38	     * @param  \Illuminate\Contracts\Auth\Authenticatable|null  $user
39	     * @param  string  $ability
40	     * @param  bool|null  $result
41	     * @param  array  $arguments
42	     */
43	    public function __construct($user, $ability, $result, $arguments)
44	    {
45	        $this->user = $user;
46	        $this->ability = $ability;
47	        $this->result = $result;
48	        $this->arguments = $arguments;
49	    }
50	}

1	<?php
2	
3	namespace Illuminate\Foundation\Auth\Access;
4	
5	use Illuminate\Contracts\Auth\Access\Gate;
6	
7	trait Authorizable
8	{
9	    /**
10	     * Determine if the entity has the given abilities.
11	     *
12	     * @param  iterable|\UnitEnum|string  $abilities
13	     * @param  mixed  $arguments
14	     * @return bool
15	     */
16	    public function can($abilities, $arguments = [])
17	    {
18	        return app(Gate::class)->forUser($this)->check($abilities, $arguments);
19	    }
20	
21	    /**
22	     * Determine if the entity has any of the given abilities.
23	     *
24	     * @param  iterable|\UnitEnum|string  $abilities
25	     * @param  mixed  $arguments
26	     * @return bool
27	     */
28	    public function canAny($abilities, $arguments = [])
29	    {
30	        return app(Gate::class)->forUser($this)->any($abilities, $arguments);
31	    }
32	
33	    /**
34	     * Determine if the entity does not have the given abilities.
35	     *
36	     * @param  iterable|\UnitEnum|string  $abilities
37	     * @param  mixed  $arguments
38	     * @return bool
39	     */
40	    public function cant($abilities, $arguments = [])
41	    {
42	        return ! $this->can($abilities, $arguments);
43	    }
44	
45	    /**
46	     * Determine if the entity does not have the given abilities.
47	     *
48	     * @param  iterable|\UnitEnum|string  $abilities
49	     * @param  mixed  $arguments
50	     * @return bool
51	     */
52	    public function cannot($abilities, $arguments = [])
53	    {
54	        return $this->cant($abilities, $arguments);
55	    }
56	}
57

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information. Let me compile my comprehensive report:

## Comprehensive Analysis: Laravel Authorization Access Control

Based on my complete examination of all files in the Auth/Access directory and related Foundation/Auth components, here's the complete architecture:

### CENTRAL EVALUATION METHOD

**`raw()` method** - THE CORE ENGINE
- **File**: `/home/bench/repos/laravel/src/Illuminate/Auth/Access/Gate.php`
- **Lines**: 434-459
- **Purpose**: Executes the actual authorization logic, calling before/after callbacks and the core ability callback
- **Returns**: `mixed` (typically `bool`, can be `Response` object, or value from after callbacks)
- **Flow**:
  1. Wraps arguments with `Arr::wrap()`
  2. Resolves the current user
  3. Calls `callBeforeCallbacks()` (lines 443-445) - can short-circuit with non-null result
  4. If no before-callback result, calls `callAuthCallback()` (lines 448) - the actual ability check
  5. Calls `callAfterCallbacks()` (lines 454-456) - for logging/side effects
  6. Dispatches `GateEvaluated` event
  7. Returns final result via `tap()`

### PUBLIC METHODS THAT CALL THE CORE EVALUATION

#### 1. **`inspect($ability, $arguments = [])`** - Lines 408-423
   - **Returns**: `Response` object (always)
   - **What it does**:
     - Calls `raw()` at line 411
     - If result is `Response` instance, returns it as-is (line 414)
     - If result is boolean, wraps in `Response::allow()` or `Response::deny()` (lines 417-419)
     - Catches `AuthorizationException` and converts to `Response` via `toResponse()` (line 421)
   - **Key**: The adapter that normalizes all results into Response objects

#### 2. **`authorize($ability, $arguments = [])`** - Lines 396-399
   - **Returns**: `Response` object (if allowed) or throws exception
   - **What it does**:
     - Calls `inspect()` at line 398
     - Calls `authorize()` method on the Response object (line 398)
   - **Side effect**: If Response is denied, throws `AuthorizationException` (see Response.php line 150)

#### 3. **`check($abilities, $arguments = [])`** - Lines 356-361
   - **Returns**: `bool`
   - **What it does**:
     - Accepts iterable or single ability
     - Loops through abilities using Collection::every()
     - For each ability calls `inspect()` (line 359)
     - Returns true only if ALL abilities have `allowed()` true
   - **Logic**: AND operation (all must pass)

#### 4. **`allows($ability, $arguments = [])`** - Lines 332-335
   - **Returns**: `bool`
   - **What it does**:
     - Simple alias/wrapper for `check()` (line 334)
     - Same semantics: returns true if ability is allowed

#### 5. **`denies($ability, $arguments = [])`** - Lines 344-347
   - **Returns**: `bool`
   - **What it does**:
     - Negation of `allows()` (line 346)
     - Returns true if ability is NOT allowed

#### 6. **`any($abilities, $arguments = [])`** - Lines 370-373
   - **Returns**: `bool`
   - **What it does**:
     - Calls `check()` for each ability (line 372)
     - Returns true if ANY ability passes
   - **Logic**: OR operation

#### 7. **`none($abilities, $arguments = [])`** - Lines 382-385
   - **Returns**: `bool`
   - **What it does**:
     - Negation of `any()` (line 384)
     - Returns true if NO abilities are allowed

#### 8. **`allowIf($condition, $message = null, $code = null)`** - Lines 146-149
   - **Returns**: `Response` object (if condition passes) or throws exception
   - **What it does**:
     - Calls `authorizeOnDemand()` (line 148) with `$allowWhenResponseIs = true`
   - **Side effect**: Throws `AuthorizationException` if condition is false

#### 9. **`denyIf($condition, $message = null, $code = null)`** - Lines 161-164
   - **Returns**: `Response` object (if condition passes) or throws exception
   - **What it does**:
     - Calls `authorizeOnDemand()` (line 163) with `$allowWhenResponseIs = false`
   - **Side effect**: Throws `AuthorizationException` if condition is true

#### 10. **`authorizeOnDemand()` (protected)** - Lines 177-192
   - Evaluates closure/condition and converts to Response
   - Calls `authorize()` on Response to potentially throw

### IMPORTANT SUPPORTING CLASSES

**Response class** - `/home/bench/repos/laravel/src/Illuminate/Auth/Access/Response.php`
- **`allowed()` method** (line 106): Returns the internal `$allowed` boolean
- **`denied()` method** (line 116): Negation of `allowed()`
- **`authorize()` method** (lines 148-157): **Throws exception if denied**, otherwise returns self
- Factory methods: `allow()`, `deny()`, `denyWithStatus()`, `denyAsNotFound()`

**AuthorizationException class** - `/home/bench/repos/laravel/src/Illuminate/Auth/Access/AuthorizationException.php`
- **`toResponse()` method** (lines 109-112): Converts exception back to Response object
- Carries Response, message, code, and HTTP status information

**HandlesAuthorization trait** - `/home/bench/repos/laravel/src/Illuminate/Auth/Access/HandlesAuthorization.php`
- **`allow()` method** (lines 14-17): Returns `Response::allow()`
- **`deny()` method** (lines 26-29): Returns `Response::deny()`
- **`denyWithStatus()` method** (lines 39-42): Returns Response with HTTP status
- **`denyAsNotFound()` method** (lines 51-54): Returns Response with 404 status

### FOUNDATION INTEGRATION CLASSES

**Authorizable trait** - `/home/bench/repos/laravel/src/Illuminate/Foundation/Auth/Access/Authorizable.php`
- User model methods (boolean return):
  - **`can($abilities, $arguments = [])`** (lines 16-19): Calls `Gate->forUser($this)->check()`
  - **`canAny($abilities, $arguments = [])`** (lines 28-31): Calls `Gate->forUser($this)->any()`
  - **`cant($abilities, $arguments = [])`** (lines 40-43): Negation of `can()`
  - **`cannot($abilities, $arguments = [])`** (lines 52-55): Alias for `cant()`

**AuthorizesRequests trait** - `/home/bench/repos/laravel/src/Illuminate/Foundation/Auth/Access/AuthorizesRequests.php`
- Controller methods (throws exception or returns Response):
  - **`authorize($ability, $arguments = [])`** (lines 21-26): Calls `Gate->authorize()`, throws exception if denied
  - **`authorizeForUser($user, $ability, $arguments = [])`** (lines 38-43): Calls `Gate->forUser($user)->authorize()`
  - **`authorizeResource($model, $parameter = null, array $options = [], $request = null)`** (lines 87-106): Middleware registration helper

### GATE FACADE

**File**: `/home/bench/repos/laravel/src/Illuminate/Support/Facades/Gate.php`
- Proxies all above methods statically via the Facade pattern
- Accessor returns: `GateContract::class`

### EVENT DISPATCH

**GateEvaluated event** - `/home/bench/repos/laravel/src/Illuminate/Auth/Access/Events/GateEvaluated.php`
- Fired after every authorization check (line 457 in Gate.php `raw()` method)
- Carries: user, ability, result (bool/null), and arguments

---

### EXECUTION CALL CHAIN SUMMARY

```
User calls one of: allow/deny/check/any/none/authorize/inspect/allowIf/denyIf
                ↓
    ┌───────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    ↓                                                                   ↓
allows/denies/check/any/none              authorize/allowIf/denyIf
    ↓                                           ↓
  inspect() ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← 
    ↓
   raw() ← CENTRAL EVALUATION ENGINE
    ↓
    callBeforeCallbacks → callAuthCallback → callAfterCallbacks
    ↓
    Returns: bool/Response/mixed
    ↓
   inspect() normalizes to Response
    ↓
   authorize() throws if Response->denied()
```

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information. Let me compile my comprehensive report:

## Comprehensive Analysis: Laravel Authorization Access Control

Based on my complete examination of all files in the Auth/Access directory and related Foundation/Auth components, here's the complete architecture:

### CENTRAL EVALUATION METHOD

**`raw()` method** - THE CORE ENGINE
- **File**: `/home/bench/repos/laravel/src/Illuminate/Auth/Access/Gate.php`
- **Lines**: 434-459
- **Purpose**: Executes the actual authorization logic, calling before/after callbacks and the core ability callback
- **Returns**: `mixed` (typically `bool`, can be `Response` object, or value from after callbacks)
- **Flow**:
  1. Wraps arguments with `Arr::wrap()`
  2. Resolves the current user
  3. Calls `callBeforeCallbacks()` (lines 443-445) - can short-circuit with non-null result
  4. If no before-callback result, calls `callAuthCallback()` (lines 448) - the actual ability check
  5. Calls `callAfterCallbacks()` (lines 454-456) - for logging/side effects
  6. Dispatches `GateEvaluated` event
  7. Returns final result via `tap()`

### PUBLIC METHODS THAT CALL THE CORE EVALUATION

#### 1. **`inspect($ability, $arguments = [])`** - Lines 408-423
   - **Returns**: `Response` object (always)
   - **What it does**:
     - Calls `raw()` at line 411
     - If result is `Response` instance, returns it as-is (line 414)
     - If result is boolean, wraps in `Response::allow()` or `Response::deny()` (lines 417-419)
     - Catches `AuthorizationException` and converts to `Response` via `toResponse()` (line 421)
   - **Key**: The adapter that normalizes all results into Response objects

#### 2. **`authorize($ability, $arguments = [])`** - Lines 396-399
   - **Returns**: `Response` object (if allowed) or throws exception
   - **What it does**:
     - Calls `inspect()` at line 398
     - Calls `authorize()` method on the Response object (line 398)
   - **Side effect**: If Response is denied, throws `AuthorizationException` (see Response.php line 150)

#### 3. **`check($abilities, $arguments = [])`** - Lines 356-361
   - **Returns**: `bool`
   - **What it does**:
     - Accepts iterable or single ability
     - Loops through abilities using Collection::every()
     - For each ability calls `inspect()` (line 359)
     - Returns true only if ALL abilities have `allowed()` true
   - **Logic**: AND operation (all must pass)

#### 4. **`allows($ability, $arguments = [])`** - Lines 332-335
   - **Returns**: `bool`
   - **What it does**:
     - Simple alias/wrapper for `check()` (line 334)
     - Same semantics: returns true if ability is allowed

#### 5. **`denies($ability, $arguments = [])`** - Lines 344-347
   - **Returns**: `bool`
   - **What it does**:
     - Negation of `allows()` (line 346)
     - Returns true if ability is NOT allowed

#### 6. **`any($abilities, $arguments = [])`** - Lines 370-373
   - **Returns**: `bool`
   - **What it does**:
     - Calls `check()` for each ability (line 372)
     - Returns true if ANY ability passes
   - **Logic**: OR operation

#### 7. **`none($abilities, $arguments = [])`** - Lines 382-385
   - **Returns**: `bool`
   - **What it does**:
     - Negation of `any()` (line 384)
     - Returns true if NO abilities are allowed

#### 8. **`allowIf($condition, $message = null, $code = null)`** - Lines 146-149
   - **Returns**: `Response` object (if condition passes) or throws exception
   - **What it does**:
     - Calls `authorizeOnDemand()` (line 148) with `$allowWhenResponseIs = true`
   - **Side effect**: Throws `AuthorizationException` if condition is false

#### 9. **`denyIf($condition, $message = null, $code = null)`** - Lines 161-164
   - **Returns**: `Response` object (if condition passes) or throws exception
   - **What it does**:
     - Calls `authorizeOnDemand()` (line 163) with `$allowWhenResponseIs = false`
   - **Side effect**: Throws `AuthorizationException` if condition is true

#### 10. **`authorizeOnDemand()` (protected)** - Lines 177-192
   - Evaluates closure/condition and converts to Response
   - Calls `authorize()` on Response to potentially throw

### IMPORTANT SUPPORTING CLASSES

**Response class** - `/home/bench/repos/laravel/src/Illuminate/Auth/Access/Response.php`
- **`allowed()` method** (line 106): Returns the internal `$allowed` boolean
- **`denied()` method** (line 116): Negation of `allowed()`
- **`authorize()` method** (lines 148-157): **Throws exception if denied**, otherwise returns self
- Factory methods: `allow()`, `deny()`, `denyWithStatus()`, `denyAsNotFound()`

**AuthorizationException class** - `/home/bench/repos/laravel/src/Illuminate/Auth/Access/AuthorizationException.php`
- **`toResponse()` method** (lines 109-112): Converts exception back to Response object
- Carries Response, message, code, and HTTP status information

**HandlesAuthorization trait** - `/home/bench/repos/laravel/src/Illuminate/Auth/Access/HandlesAuthorization.php`
- **`allow()` method** (lines 14-17): Returns `Response::allow()`
- **`deny()` method** (lines 26-29): Returns `Response::deny()`
- **`denyWithStatus()` method** (lines 39-42): Returns Response with HTTP status
- **`denyAsNotFound()` method** (lines 51-54): Returns Response with 404 status

### FOUNDATION INTEGRATION CLASSES

**Authorizable trait** - `/home/bench/repos/laravel/src/Illuminate/Foundation/Auth/Access/Authorizable.php`
- User model methods (boolean return):
  - **`can($abilities, $arguments = [])`** (lines 16-19): Calls `Gate->forUser($this)->check()`
  - **`canAny($abilities, $arguments = [])`** (lines 28-31): Calls `Gate->forUser($this)->any()`
  - **`cant($abilities, $arguments = [])`** (lines 40-43): Negation of `can()`
  - **`cannot($abilities, $arguments = [])`** (lines 52-55): Alias for `cant()`

**AuthorizesRequests trait** - `/home/bench/repos/laravel/src/Illuminate/Foundation/Auth/Access/AuthorizesRequests.php`
- Controller methods (throws exception or returns Response):
  - **`authorize($ability, $arguments = [])`** (lines 21-26): Calls `Gate->authorize()`, throws exception if denied
  - **`authorizeForUser($user, $ability, $arguments = [])`** (lines 38-43): Calls `Gate->forUser($user)->authorize()`
  - **`authorizeResource($model, $parameter = null, array $options = [], $request = null)`** (lines 87-106): Middleware registration helper

### GATE FACADE

**File**: `/home/bench/repos/laravel/src/Illuminate/Support/Facades/Gate.php`
- Proxies all above methods statically via the Facade pattern
- Accessor returns: `GateContract::class`

### EVENT DISPATCH

**GateEvaluated event** - `/home/bench/repos/laravel/src/Illuminate/Auth/Access/Events/GateEvaluated.php`
- Fired after every authorization check (line 457 in Gate.php `raw()` method)
- Carries: user, ability, result (bool/null), and arguments

---

### EXECUTION CALL CHAIN SUMMARY

```
User calls one of: allow/deny/check/any/none/authorize/inspect/allowIf/denyIf
                ↓
    ┌───────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    ↓                                                                   ↓
allows/denies/check/any/none              authorize/allowIf/denyIf
    ↓                                           ↓
  inspect() ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← 
    ↓
   raw() ← CENTRAL EVALUATION ENGINE
    ↓
    callBeforeCallbacks → callAuthCallback → callAfterCallbacks
    ↓
    Returns: bool/Response/mixed
    ↓
   inspect() normalizes to Response
    ↓
   authorize() throws if Response->denied()
```

file.content:
<?php

namespace Illuminate\Foundation\Auth\Access;

use Illuminate\Contracts\Auth\Access\Gate;
use Illuminate\Support\Str;

use function Illuminate\Support\enum_value;

trait AuthorizesRequests
{
    /**
     * Authorize a given action for the current user.
     *
     * @param  mixed  $ability
     * @param  mixed  $arguments
     * @return \Illuminate\Auth\Access\Response
     *
     * @throws \Illuminate\Auth\Access\AuthorizationException
     */
    public function authorize($ability, $arguments = [])
    {
        [$ability, $arguments] = $this->parseAbilityAndArguments($ability, $arguments);

        return app(Gate::class)->authorize($ability, $arguments);
    }

    /**
     * Authorize a given action for a user.
     *
     * @param  \Illuminate\Contracts\Auth\Authenticatable|mixed  $user
     * @param  mixed  $ability
     * @param  mixed  $arguments
     * @return \Illuminate\Auth\Access\Response
     *
     * @throws \Illuminate\Auth\Access\AuthorizationException
     */
    public function authorizeForUser($user, $ability, $arguments = [])
    {
        [$ability, $arguments] = $this->parseAbilityAndArguments($ability, $arguments);

        return app(Gate::class)->forUser($user)->authorize($ability, $arguments);
    }

    /**
     * Guesses the ability's name if it wasn't provided.
     *
     * @param  mixed  $ability
     * @param  mixed  $arguments
     * @return array
     */
    protected function parseAbilityAndArguments($ability, $arguments)
    {
        $ability = enum_value($ability);

        if (is_string($ability) && ! str_contains($ability, '\\')) {
            return [$ability, $arguments];
        }

        $method = debug_backtrace(DEBUG_BACKTRACE_IGNORE_ARGS, 3)[2]['function'];

        return [$this->normalizeGuessedAbilityName($method), $ability];
    }

    /**
     * Normalize the ability name that has been guessed from the method name.
     *
     * @param  string  $ability
     * @return string
     */
    protected function normalizeGuessedAbilityName($ability)
    {
        $map = $this->resourceAbilityMap();

        return $map[$ability] ?? $ability;
    }

    /**
     * Authorize a resource action based on the incoming request.
     *
     * @param  string|array  $model
     * @param  string|array|null  $parameter
     * @param  array  $options
     * @param  \Illuminate\Http\Request|null  $request
     * @return void
     */
    public function authorizeResource($model, $parameter = null, array $options = [], $request = null)
    {
        $model = is_array($model) ? implode(',', $model) : $model;

        $parameter = is_array($parameter) ? implode(',', $parameter) : $parameter;

        $parameter = $parameter ?: Str::snake(class_basename($model));

        $middleware = [];

        foreach ($this->resourceAbilityMap() as $method => $ability) {
            $modelName = in_array($method, $this->resourceMethodsWithoutModels()) ? $model : $parameter;

            $middleware["can:{$ability},{$modelName}"][] = $method;
        }

        foreach ($middleware as $middlewareName => $methods) {
            $this->middleware($middlewareName, $options)->only($methods);
        }
    }

    /**
     * Get the map of resource methods to ability names.
     *
     * @return array<string, string>
     */
    protected function resourceAbilityMap()
    {
        return [
            'index' => 'viewAny',
            'show' => 'view',
            'create' => 'create',
            'store' => 'create',
            'edit' => 'update',
            'update' => 'update',
            'destroy' => 'delete',
        ];
    }

    /**
     * Get the list of resource methods which do not have model parameters.
     *
     * @return list<string>
     */
    protected function resourceMethodsWithoutModels()
    {
        return ['index', 'create', 'store'];
    }
}

content.thinking: Now I have a clear picture of the authorization system. Let me provide a concise, well-structured explanation to the user.

content.text:
Here's how the authorization system is structured, traced from the core outward.

---

## The central check: `Gate::raw()`

`src/Illuminate/Auth/Access/Gate.php:434`

This is the single point through which every ability evaluation passes. It:

1. Resolves the current user (`resolveUser()`)
2. Runs **before-callbacks** — any non-`null` return short-circuits the rest
3. Falls through to `callAuthCallback()` — dispatches to the registered closure, a `Policy` method, or a guessed policy method
4. Runs **after-callbacks** (logging, side-effects; their return value replaces the result)
5. Fires the `GateEvaluated` event and returns whatever was produced (`bool`, a `Response` object, or `null`)

`raw()` is `public` but is not the typical entry point — callers go through `inspect()`.

---

## The normalizing adapter: `Gate::inspect()`

`Gate.php:408`

Wraps `raw()` and **always** returns a `Response` object:

```
raw() returns bool   →  Response::allow()  or  $defaultDenialResponse / Response::deny()
raw() returns Response →  passed through as-is
raw() throws AuthorizationException  →  $e->toResponse()
```

Every higher-level public method is built on top of `inspect()`.

---

## Public callers and what they do with the verdict

| Method | File:line | Returns | Throws? | Logic |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `inspect($ability)` | `Gate.php:408` | `Response` | never | normalizes `raw()` → `Response` |
| `authorize($ability)` | `Gate.php:396` | `Response` (allowed only) | yes — `AuthorizationException` | calls `inspect()->authorize()` |
| `check($abilities)` | `Gate.php:356` | `bool` | no | `every` ability passes `inspect()->allowed()` (AND) |
| `allows($ability)` | `Gate.php:332` | `bool` | no | alias for `check()` |
| `denies($ability)` | `Gate.php:344` | `bool` | no | `!allows()` |
| `any($abilities)` | `Gate.php:370` | `bool` | no | `contains` ability passes `check()` (OR) |
| `none($abilities)` | `Gate.php:382` | `bool` | no | `!any()` |
| `allowIf($condition)` | `Gate.php:146` | `Response` | yes | `authorizeOnDemand(…, allowWhen=true)` |
| `denyIf($condition)` | `Gate.php:161` | `Response` | yes | `authorizeOnDemand(…, allowWhen=false)` |

`allowIf`/`denyIf` bypass `raw()` entirely — they evaluate a bare `bool` or `Closure` directly in `authorizeOnDemand()` (`Gate.php:177`) and wrap the result in a `Response`, then call `->authorize()` on it.

---

## Where `AuthorizationException` is actually thrown

`Response::authorize()` at `Response.php:148` — that is the single throw site. Every path that needs to abort-on-deny ultimately calls this method:

```
Gate::authorize()  →  inspect()->authorize()           (on the Response)
AuthorizesRequests::authorize()  →  Gate::authorize()  (controller trait)
allowIf / denyIf  →  authorizeOnDemand()->authorize()
```

The exception carries the `Response` (and optional HTTP status), so the exception handler can inspect it.

---

## Controller-level entry points (`AuthorizesRequests` trait)

`src/Illuminate/Foundation/Auth/Access/AuthorizesRequests.php`

Controllers use this trait, which adds two throwing wrappers:

- `authorize($ability, $arguments)` — resolves `Gate` from the container, calls `Gate::authorize()`, throws on denial (:21)
- `authorizeForUser($user, $ability, $arguments)` — same, but scoped to an explicit user via `Gate::forUser()` (:38)
- `authorizeResource($model, …)` — does **not** call `raw()`; it registers `can:ability,model` middleware for every resourceful route (:87)

---

## User model entry points (`Authorizable` trait)

`src/Illuminate/Foundation/Auth/Access/Authorizable.php`

The `User` model gets boolean helpers that scope the gate to `$this`:

- `$user->can($ability)` → `Gate::forUser($this)->check()`
- `$user->canAny($abilities)` → `Gate::forUser($this)->any()`
- `$user->cant()` / `cannot()` → `!can()`

These never throw; they return plain booleans and are the right choice for branching logic in business code.

---

## Summary of the call chain

```
Controller $this->authorize()          $user->can()         Gate::allows/check/any
           ↓                              ↓                         ↓
    Gate::authorize()              Gate::check()             Gate::check()
           ↓                              ↓                         ↓
    Gate::inspect()  ←────────────────────┴─────────────────────────┘
           ↓
       Gate::raw()          ← THE CORE (before → authCallback → after → event)
           ↓
    Response object
           ↓
  authorize() on Response   ← only throw site
```

The key design rule: **`raw()` produces the verdict; `inspect()` normalizes it; `Response::authorize()` enforces it.** Whether a caller wants a boolean, a `Response`, or an exception is entirely determined by which layer it stops at.
